mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with t-statistic greater than 2.0, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 negatively.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of months where unemployment rises above the 0.3pp threshold, or signal fails to separate bull and bear months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive when both retail sales and housing starts decline, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative when industrial production accelerates during tightening periods, or regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.5% annually forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing recession probabilities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally when inflation breaches 3.5%, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns decline when payroll growth exceeds 150000, or the payroll signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation rather than final demand growth, triggering negative equity returns as earnings revisions turn downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% while RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: S&P 500 advances when GDP outpaces retail sales, or the divergence signal fails to predict return direction with accuracy above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins and drive negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive when industrial production falls during recovery phases, or the regime filter adds no incremental predictive value over baseline models.
